_comment: Sidecar allowlist for update_label_db / lint_pret_labels (fidelity plan Stage 2.1).
relocated_files: every translated label of <pret file> deliberately lives at <port_file>.
relocated_labels: per-label relocations where one pret file split across port files.
DRAFT (Session H 2026-07-07): entries below bless pre-existing relocations found by the
first full-tree scan; they predate the mirror rule. Flagged for user review — deleting an
entry makes the linter fire on it again.

engine/menus/link_menu.asm.why: The 18 labels are the COLOSSEUM cup ELIGIBILITY rules (PokeCup/PikaCup/PetitCup and their 15 verdict routines), reached only as data through PointerTable_f5488. They are a different concern from the two menus with a different dependency set: they scan the party, read the Pokedex entry pointers (link_cups.o depends on assets/dex_entries.inc) and call GetMonName, and they touch no window, tilemap or menu-driver state at all — pret bundles them with the menus only because they share a ROM bank. Reviewed on the merits 2026-07-14 (menu-fidelity row 20 part 2), NOT kept as boilerplate: no pret label is forked, renamed or duplicated across the split (link_cups.asm externs the Colosseum*Text wrappers from link_menu.asm and prints them itself, exactly as pret does), faithdiff/label_status key on labels rather than files, and the discovery trail runs both ways (each file's header names the other and the pret file). Merging back would put ~1500 lines in one file, which is the size at which this very review had to be split across two iterations.

engine/menus/start_sub_menus.asm.why: DrawTrainerInfo + its 5 TrainerInfo_* helpers are one self-contained SCREEN (VRAM tile loads, Red's front pic, box drawing, present/teardown); pret bundles them into start_sub_menus.asm only because they share a ROM bank with the six unrelated StartMenu_* dispatchers, which the flat port has no reason to reproduce. Reviewed on the merits 2026-07-13 (menu-fidelity row 10), NOT kept as boilerplate: no pret label is forked, renamed or duplicated across the split, faithdiff/label_status key on labels rather than files, and the discovery trail runs both ways (start_sub_menus.asm:116-119 externs name trainer_card.asm; trainer_card.asm's header names pret's file). Merging back would push the dispatch file past 1700 lines for zero cross-reference gain.

DisplayTwoOptionMenu.why: Deliberate, and NOT a file-level relocation (the old entry keyed the whole of pret engine/menus/text_box.asm to yes_no.asm, which was simply false: that file's other labels do live in dos_port/src/engine/menus/text_box.asm). Only this routine moves. Reason: in the port DisplayTwoOptionMenu is not a standalone routine. pret receives the box position in a register triple (b=cursor Y, c=cursor X, hl=tilemap address of the border); the port's window compositor needs GB TILE coordinates, not a tilemap address, so the position now arrives in yn_box_col/yn_box_row -- private .bss written by the home/yes_no.asm entry points -- and the box is placed and dropped through yn_show_window/yn_mirror/yn_teardown, also private to this file. Hosting the routine in its mirrored path would export three .bss cells and three helpers across a file boundary purely to satisfy the mirror rule, splitting one cohesive unit in two. Its only out-of-file caller, DisplayTextBoxID_'s TWO_OPTION_MENU dispatch, is an extern either way. Re-justified by hand at menu-fidelity row 5 (2026-07-13).

PrintMonType.why: Row 22 created the real mirror (dos_port/src/engine/battle/print_type.asm) and put pret's move-type half there (PrintMoveType/PrintType/PrintType_), so the old file-level blanket entry for engine/battle/print_type.asm is deleted. PrintMonType alone is still elsewhere: its port body was written INTO status_screen.asm, fused with EraseType2Text (which pret keeps as a separate label and the port therefore reports as `missing`), and it reads the status screen's own layout offsets. Relocating it is a real, separable change to status_screen.asm — see menu-fidelity finding M-119 — not a fact about this file, so it is allowlisted per-label, not per-file.

suppress.why: documented DEVIATION integration stub: the one call (predef InitPlayerData2) is load-bearing — seeds party/bag/money on new game; cutscene itself is script-engine plan work
documented DEVIATION integration stub: arms menu vars (wMaxMenuItem etc.) so PCMainMenu dispatch runs on defined state — minimal contract, no box draw

DECISION-FOR-USER (Session H): full faithful body lives in core_stubs.asm because misc.asm is check-only and its GetName dep isn't link-ready; should move to a non-stub file or ride misc.asm's promotion
known interim: core_stubs.asm body is linked; misc.asm's is check-only (BATTLE_SRCS). Resolve with the non_ret_stub decision above
known interim: overworld_stubs.asm ret-stub is linked; real body in pikachu.asm is check-only (HOME_CHECK_SRCS). Delete stub when pikachu.asm is promoted (Wave 9/M9.1)

known interim (OW-5.1): overworld_stubs.asm ret-stub is linked (EnterMap's caller); faithful body in player_animations.asm is check-only (HOME_CHECK_SRCS). Delete stub when player_animations.asm is promoted to GAME_SRCS at OW-7.2 (same pattern as SpawnPikachu)

known interim (wild-live promotion): home_stubs.asm holds a documented ret-stub that is LINKED; home/text_script.asm holds the real body but stays check-only (HOME_CHECK_SRCS) because its link closure is 15 symbols deep and 8 of them are DisplayTextID special-case dispatch targets owned by the script-engine session. The stub's only linked caller is TryDoWildEncounter's repel-wore-off branch, unreachable until item USE lands. Retire by linking text_script.asm and DELETING the stub — the duplicate global is then a loud link error, not a silent shadow.
